meta.version_quirks: Emerald merges Ruby and Sapphire content, adds the Battle Frontier, lets you catch both Groudon and Kyogre, introduces a fully animated overworld for Pokemon sprites, and features expanded contests and a refined story focused on Rayquaza.

starters.name: Treecko
strengths: Fastest of the three starters at every stage
Strong against Roxanne, Wattson (with Bullet Seed), Juan/Wallace, and Drake's Kingdra
Learns Leaf Blade naturally as Grovyle (Lv 29) which is one of the best moves in Gen 3
Pure Grass typing means only one weakness multiplier per type
weaknesses: Bad matchup against Flannery, Winona, Phoebe, Glacia, and most of the Elite Four
Movepool outside of Grass is shallow on the cartridge (mostly Crunch, Dragon Claw via TM)
Frail defensive stats; gets one-shot by stray Ice or Flying moves

early_game: Bullet Seed (TM09 from Cycling Road shop)

starters.best_for_new_players_rating: 6/10 - rewarding but punishing. Sceptile demands smart switching.
starters.fun_trivia: Sceptile was the first pure Grass-type starter final with a base 120 Speed stat, and its design was inspired by the Carolina anole lizard. In Gen 6 it gained a Mega Evolution that turned it Grass/Dragon.
starters.ability: Overgrow (boosts Grass moves at <1/3 HP). Gen 3 has no hidden abilities.
starters.best_nature: Modest (+SpA, -Atk) for Sceptile's special movepool, or Timid (+Spe, -Atk) to outpace 130-base-Speed threats
starters.stat_focus_lv100: 120 Spe / 105 SpA / 85 Atk / 70 HP - the speed tier IS the build
starters.evolution_timing: Don't delay - Grovyle at Lv 16 unlocks Leaf Blade at Lv 29; both evolutions are timing-critical for the Norman push.

starters.name: Torchic
starters.signature_evolution: Torchic -> Combusken (Lv 16, gains Fighting type) -> Blaziken (Lv 36)
strengths: Becomes Fire/Fighting at Combusken, which is incredible for a 4th badge run
Double Kick at Lv 16 trivializes Roxanne's gym
Sky Uppercut as Combusken hits Phoebe, Sidney, and Glacia
Blaze Kick + Sky Uppercut covers most of Hoenn's threats
weaknesses: Slow until Blaziken; outsped by many gym aces
Awful matchup against Wallace/Juan and Tate & Liza's Lunatone
4x weak to nothing but stacks Water/Ground/Flying/Psychic weaknesses
starters.best_for_new_players_rating: 9/10 - the easiest run in Emerald thanks to dual STAB and great coverage.
starters.fun_trivia: Blaziken was the first Fire/Fighting starter, kicking off a Gen-long tradition (Infernape, Emboar, Cinderace). It became the first Pokemon ever given a Mega Evolution in X/Y.
starters.ability: Blaze (boosts Fire moves at <1/3 HP). Gen 3 has no hidden abilities.
starters.best_nature: Adamant (+Atk, -SpA) - Blaziken's physical movepool (Sky Uppercut, Blaze Kick, Earthquake) outshines its special
starters.stat_focus_lv100: 120 Atk / 110 SpA / 80 Spe / 80 HP - mixed offense, mediocre defenses
starters.evolution_timing: Combusken at Lv 16 unlocks Double Kick (gym 1 trivial). Combusken learns Sky Uppercut at Lv 36 - the SAME level as Blaziken evolution, so DON'T cancel evolution; you save no moves and lose the stats.

starters.name: Mudkip
starters.signature_evolution: Mudkip -> Marshtomp (Lv 16, gains Ground type) -> Swampert (Lv 36)
strengths: Becomes Water/Ground at Marshtomp - immune to Electric, only one weakness (4x Grass)
Solo's Roxanne, Wattson, Flannery, and Tate & Liza without breaking a sweat
Highest bulk of any starter; great for new players
Learns Earthquake, Surf, and Ice Beam by TM for full coverage
weaknesses: 4x weak to Grass moves - watch for Winona's no Grass, but Drake's Salamence and wild Ludicolo can sting
Predictable; many trainers carry Razor Leaf or Giga Drain
starters.fun_trivia: Swampert is one of only three Water/Ground Pokemon families in Gen 3 (with Whiscash and Quagsire). The 'So I herd u liek mudkipz' meme originated from a 4chan thread in 2007 and helped cement Mudkip in pop culture.
starters.ability: Torrent (boosts Water moves at <1/3 HP). Gen 3 has no hidden abilities.
starters.best_nature: Adamant (+Atk, -SpA) - Swampert is a physical attacker (Earthquake / Waterfall / Rock Slide); Modest only if running Surf-Ice Beam build
egg_moves: Avalanche (Wooper chain - Gen 4 only, N/A in Emerald)
starters.stat_focus_lv100: 100 HP / 110 Atk / 90 Def / 85 SpA / 60 Spe - bulky physical wallbreaker
starters.evolution_timing: Marshtomp at Lv 16 picks up Mud Shot, the gym 3 sweep. Swampert at Lv 36 is automatic - no reason to delay.

gym_leaders.name: Roxanne
gym_leaders.strategy: Nosepass has Harden and Rock Tomb. The Rock Tomb speed drop is what kills slow physical attackers, so prefer Special Attackers. A Magnemite caught at Cave of Origin route is too high level here, but a Lotad from Route 102 evolved to Lombre is excellent. Avoid using Torchic alone unless it has Double Kick (Lv 16, so train one extra level first).
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Boosts Attack stat in battle and lets you use HM01 Cut outside of battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Backtrack to Petalburg Woods after the gym to grab a Shroomish - it evolves into Breloom with Mach Punch by Lv 23.
gym_leaders.notable_abilities_items: Geodude has Sturdy (cannot be one-shot from full HP). Nosepass has Sturdy. No held items.
gym_leaders.rematch_note: Vanilla Emerald has NO gym leader rematches. Match Call/PokeNav doesn't connect to leaders. Rematches were added retroactively in ORAS.

gym_leaders.name: Brawly

team.key_move: Bulk Up + Arm Thrust + Vital Throw

gym_leaders.strategy: Makuhita uses Bulk Up to snowball. Don't let it set up - hit it on turn 1 with a strong Flying or Psychic attack. Taillow's Wing Attack and Quick Attack make this gym trivial. Beware Vital Throw, which never misses.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Pokemon up to Lv 30 obey, and you can use HM05 Flash outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Catch a Magnemite in New Mauville later - useless for Brawly, but you can grab a Whismur in Rusturf Tunnel that evolves into Loudred and learns Stomp.
gym_leaders.notable_abilities_items: Makuhita has Guts (boosts Atk if statused). No held items. Watch the Bulk Up snowball.
gym_leaders.rematch_note: No vanilla rematch. ORAS upgrades Makuhita to Hariyama with Sitrus Berry.

gym_leaders.name: Wattson
gym_leaders.strategy: A Ground-type fully neutralizes Wattson. Marshtomp is the cleanest sweep in the game. Watch out for Voltorb's Self-Destruct - swap to a Rock or Steel type for the Boom turn. Magneton is Steel/Electric, so its only weakness here is Ground/Fire/Fighting.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Boosts Defense in battle and enables HM06 Rock Smash outside battle.
gym_leaders.tm_given: TM34 Shock Wave (a never-miss Electric attack)
gym_leaders.tip: After defeating Wattson, talk to him again - he gives you a Thunder Stone-related sidequest to clear out New Mauville's generators, rewarding TM24 Thunderbolt.
gym_leaders.notable_abilities_items: Magneton has Sturdy. Voltorb has Soundproof or Static. Manectric has Static (30% paralysis on contact). No held items.

gym_leaders.name: Flannery
gym_leaders.strategy: Torkoal opens with Overheat which lowers its own Special Attack two stages, so you can stall through it. Watch for Attract, which neuters male attackers - bring a female Pokemon or Yellow Flute to break it. Numel and Camerupt are Fire/Ground, so Water moves do quadruple damage. Slugma is pure Fire.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Lets you use HM04 Strength outside battle and boosts Speed in battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Hot Springs in Lavaridge heal your team for free - park here while training. The old woman in town gives you an Egg containing Wynaut.
gym_leaders.notable_abilities_items: Camerupt has Magma Armor (no freeze). Torkoal has White Smoke (immune to stat drops, ignores its own Overheat penalty? - no, it still drops). No held items.

gym_leaders.name: Norman

team.key_move: Facade + Slack Off + Yawn (every other turn due to Truant)

gym_leaders.strategy: Slaking has Truant - it loafs every other turn. Use the loafing turn to set up Bulk Up, heal, or switch in a wall. Hariyama with Vital Throw and Belly Drum is the textbook counter. Don't put a Ghost type in - he carries Faint Attack. Slaking's Yawn forces switches; bring an Awakening or use a Pokemon with Insomnia/Vital Spirit.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Lets all Pokemon up to Lv 70 obey and enables HM03 Surf outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Norman is your father in this game - the dialogue around the gym fight is some of the best in the franchise. Mom calls you on the PokeNav after.
gym_leaders.notable_abilities_items: Slaking has Truant (every other turn loafs - exploit it). Vigoroth has Vital Spirit (immune to sleep). Linoone has Pickup. No held items in vanilla.
gym_leaders.rematch_note: No vanilla rematch. Norman is the most-requested rematch - ORAS adds a stronger Slaking + Slaking pair via DexNav.

gym_leaders.name: Winona
gym_leaders.strategy: Altaria is Dragon/Flying, so Ice Beam is a one-shot. Skarmory is Steel/Flying - hit it with Fire or Electric, NOT Rock (it resists). Pelipper is doubly weak to Electric. The whole gym is Electric food - Manectric or Magneton solo it. Watch for Altaria's Dragon Dance.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Pokemon up to Lv 80 obey, and you can use HM02 Fly outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: After Winona, head to Mt. Pyre next - this is where the team plot really kicks off and you'll need Fly to keep up with the legendary chase.
gym_leaders.notable_abilities_items: Skarmory has Keen Eye/Sturdy. Altaria has Natural Cure (heals status on switch). Pelipper has Keen Eye. No held items.

gym_leaders.name: Tate & Liza
gym_leaders.battle_format: DOUBLE BATTLE - this is the only mandatory double-battle gym in the series
gym_leaders.strategy: Lead with two Dark or Ghost types. Sharpedo's Crunch is a one-shot on either ace. Earthquake hits both opponents - perfect partners are Flygon, Camerupt, or Swampert paired with a Flying ally to dodge it. Beware: Solrock has Sunny Day + SolarBeam combo, and Lunatone has Hypnosis. Bring Awakenings.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Pokemon up to Lv 90 obey, and you can use HM08 Dive outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: Pick up the Sun Stone from the meteorite-loving NPCs near the Space Center after the gym - it's the only one in the early-mid game.
gym_leaders.notable_abilities_items: Solrock and Lunatone have Levitate (Ground-immune). Claydol has Levitate. Xatu has Synchronize (mirrors status to attacker). No held items in vanilla.

gym_leaders.name: Wallace

team.warning: Whiscash is Water/Ground - Grass moves do 4x

gym_leaders.strategy: Note: in vanilla Emerald, Wallace is the gym leader and Juan is replaced. (In Ruby/Sapphire it was Wallace.) Milotic has Recover + Marvel Scale, so it's spongy. Status it with Toxic or burn, then chunk with Electric or Grass. Whiscash is doubly weak to Grass. Don't bring Mudkip line solo - Milotic outclasses it.
gym_leaders.badge_perk: Pokemon of all levels obey, and you can use HM07 Waterfall outside battle.
gym_leaders.tip: After Wallace's gym, you trigger the Sootopolis Kyogre/Groudon climax. Stock up on Ultra Balls, Max Repels, and Revives at the Pokemart before entering Cave of Origin.
gym_leaders.notable_abilities_items: Milotic has Marvel Scale (1.5x Def when statused - so DON'T paralyze it; burn or sleep instead, contradicting the early-card advice). Whiscash has Oblivious. Sealeo has Thick Fat. No held items.
gym_leaders.rematch_note: No vanilla rematch. Note: in R/S Wallace was the gym leader; in Emerald he is also gym leader AND replaces Steven as Champion.

elite_four.name: Sidney
elite_four.key_strategy: Hariyama with Brick Break or Cross Chop, Blaziken with Sky Uppercut, or Heracross with Megahorn rolls this whole team. Watch for Swagger - it raises your Attack but confuses you. Bring a Persim Berry or use a Special Attacker. Shiftry's Fake Out forces a flinch turn 1, so don't lead with your sweeper at full HP commit.
elite_four.abilities_items_note: Mightyena has Intimidate (-1 Atk to your lead). Absol has Pressure (drains 2 PP per move). Cacturne has Sand Veil. No held items.
elite_four.suggested_archetype: Bring 2 Fighters (Hariyama + Blaziken or Heracross). Cover Cacturne with a Flying or Fire move.

elite_four.name: Phoebe
elite_four.key_strategy: Dark moves are super effective AND Ghost types take neutral damage from Normal (they're immune to Normal but Crunch/Bite are Dark, which deals super effective). Sharpedo's Crunch, Houndoom's Crunch, or Absol's Bite/Crunch all dismantle her. Sableye is the trickiest - it has no weakness on its dual typing (Dark/Ghost). Use Earthquake or wear it down with Toxic. Avoid Psychic-types entirely - Phoebe's Ghosts have Crunch/Faint Attack.
elite_four.abilities_items_note: Dusclops has Pressure. Banette has Insomnia (sleep-immune). Sableye has Keen Eye. Watch Will-O-Wisp from Banette. No held items.
elite_four.suggested_archetype: Lead with a fast Dark attacker (Sharpedo/Absol Crunch). Bench any Psychic. Bring Toxic for Sableye.

elite_four.name: Glacia
elite_four.key_strategy: All her Pokemon are pure Ice except Walrein (Ice/Water). Blaziken with Blaze Kick or Flamethrower obliterates the gym. Hariyama with Brick Break is also fantastic. Watch out for Walrein's Sheer Cold - it's a one-hit KO move. Walrein is doubly weak to Electric due to Water typing. Manectric or Magneton can revenge.
elite_four.abilities_items_note: Walrein has Thick Fat (resists Fire and Ice further). Sealeo has Thick Fat. Glalie has Inner Focus. No held items.
elite_four.suggested_archetype: Lead Fire/Fighting (Blaziken). Bench Manectric/Magneton for Walrein revenge. Avoid Mudkip line - Walrein walls it.

elite_four.name: Drake
elite_four.key_strategy: Ice Beam is your best friend. Walrein, Glalie, or Lapras (traded) work great. Salamence and Altaria are doubly weak to Ice (Dragon/Flying). Beware Earthquake on Flygon and Salamence - don't lead with Magneton. Kingdra is Dragon/Water and only weak to Dragon, so save it for last with a Dragon Claw user. Glacia's stolen Walrein from your prior fight should still have full Ice Beam PP if you slept through her with Soft-Boiled.
elite_four.abilities_items_note: Salamence has Intimidate. Flygon has Levitate (Ground-immune). Altaria has Natural Cure. Shelgon has Rock Head (no recoil from Double-Edge). No held items.
elite_four.suggested_archetype: TWO Ice Beam users minimum (Walrein + Glalie, or borrow from your Lapras). Pack a Rock Slide for Altaria/Salamence pivot.

elite_four.name: Wallace
elite_four.title: Champion
elite_four.weakness: Mostly Electric/Grass, but Ludicolo (Water/Grass) is only weak to Bug/Flying/Poison
elite_four.key_strategy: Manectric or Raikou-tier Electric attackers shred most of his team. The catch is Ludicolo - bring Sceptile, Beautifly, or a Crobat to handle it. Whiscash is Water/Ground - 4x weak to Grass. Gyarados is 4x weak to Electric. Milotic with Recover + Marvel Scale is the wall - status it (paralyze with Thunder Wave) before chunking with Electric. Wallace is the only Champion in the franchise to specialize in Water.
elite_four.abilities_items_note: Milotic has Marvel Scale (do NOT paralyze - it boosts her Def). Gyarados has Intimidate. Tentacruel has Liquid Ooze (drain moves hurt the user). Ludicolo has Swift Swim. No held items.
elite_four.suggested_archetype: Electric core (Manectric + Magneton OR Raikou-tier) for 4 of 6. Sceptile or a Bug Flying for Ludicolo. Lapras/Walrein for Whiscash via Ice Beam.

legendaries.name: Groudon
legendaries.location: Terra Cave (roaming/anchor cave that appears in a random route after the Sootopolis storyline)
access_requirements: Beat the Elite Four
Speak to Professor Cozmo and check the news on TV in any Pokemon Center
Terra Cave's location rotates - check the Hoenn news report each day
legendaries.catching_tips: False Swipe with a Gallade/Sceptile to leave 1 HP, then status it (Hypnosis from Gardevoir or Thunder Wave). Groudon has Drought ability which boosts Fire moves and weakens Water moves, so don't try to chip with Surf. Use Ultra Balls or a Timer Ball after 10+ turns. Has Earthquake, Fire Blast, Hyper Beam, Bulk Up - strong and bulky.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Timer Ball (after turn 10) or Ultra Ball; Dusk Ball if you can wake it up at night
legendaries.save_routine: Save IN the doorway of Terra Cave. Encounter, attempt capture, soft-reset (A+B+Start+Select) on a bad result.
legendaries.ohko_warning: Groudon's Hyper Beam can KO False-Swipers - bring 200+ HP buffer. Drought blocks Surf chip damage.

legendaries.name: Kyogre
legendaries.location: Marine Cave (roaming/anchor cave on a random water route after Sootopolis)
access_requirements: Beat the Elite Four
Marine Cave appears on a random water route; check Hoenn TV/news
Surf required to reach it
legendaries.catching_tips: Has Drizzle which forces rain, doubling its own Water move damage and weakening Fire. Don't bring a Fire attacker. Use a Whismur/Loudred with Sing for sleep or a Gengar with Hypnosis. Its moveset includes Sheer Cold (OHKO!), so save before engaging. Same Timer Ball strategy as Groudon.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Timer Ball or Net Ball (it's part Water)
legendaries.save_routine: Save right outside Marine Cave, then approach. Soft-reset for IVs/Nature.
legendaries.ohko_warning: Sheer Cold is Kyogre's anti-False-Swipe insurance - 30% accuracy but it WILL roll eventually. Bring backup.

legendaries.name: Rayquaza
legendaries.location: Sky Pillar (Route 131, accessible after the Sootopolis crisis)
access_requirements: Reach Sky Pillar via Mach Bike navigation - the cracked floors require speed
Climb 5 floors of bike puzzles
Story-mandatory encounter to settle Groudon vs Kyogre, but if you knock it out you can re-encounter it after Hall of Fame
legendaries.catching_tips: Highest base stat total of any Pokemon you can catch in the cartridge (680). Carries Outrage, Extreme Speed, Fly, Rest. Don't let Outrage lock it in if you're using a Dragon - the recoil-confusion is brutal. False Swipe doesn't work (Outrage hits hard back). Lower with strong neutral moves, then status (paralyze ideally - it can't sleep with Rest unless you remove Chesto Berry). Save before engaging.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Timer Ball is mathematically best after ~30 turns; Ultra Ball as backup
legendaries.save_routine: Save on the floor BELOW Rayquaza, then climb up. The Sky Pillar bike puzzle is fast; soft-reset is cheap.
legendaries.ohko_warning: Outrage hits 90 BP + STAB + Dragon = ~250 damage to neutral attackers. Don't park a Dragon-type as bait.

legendaries.name: Latios
legendaries.location: Roams Hoenn after the Hoenn TV news event with Mom (post-7th badge)
access_requirements: Watch the news at home with your mom after getting the 7th badge

Choose 'Blue' in the news for Latios (default in Emerald is Latios; Latias if you choose differently in the male/female protagonist version - actually in Emerald it's locked to Latios for male protagonist and Latias for female, with the other obtainable via the Eon Ticket event)
Eon Ticket (Mystery Gift event item) lets you sail to Southern Island for the OTHER Latios/Latias
legendaries.catching_tips: Roaming legendary - it flees on turn 1. Use Mean Look with Crobat (Lv 30+ learns it via breeding/move tutor) or Block. Repels block lower-level encounters so you only run into Latios when biking. Use Master Ball if you want to skip the chase entirely - this is the most justified Master Ball in the game.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Master Ball (highly recommended), or Net Ball after Mean Look
legendaries.save_routine: Save before answering Mom's TV question. The roamer's IVs/Nature are LOCKED at this moment - soft-reset before answering for a perfect Lati.
legendaries.talked_fled_note: If you 'lose' the encounter (it flees, you switch trainers, etc.) the Pokeball-with-fled-status STAYS in the world but the Lati is gone for that route until you re-trigger PokeNav tracking. Don't waste status moves - lock it with Mean Look turn 1.
legendaries.repel_trick: Walk a route with Repel active and a Lv 41+ lead. Repel blocks all wild encounters lower than your lead - so the Lv 40 Lati is the ONLY thing you can run into.

legendaries.name: Latias
legendaries.location: Same as Latios - which one roams depends on your news choice and Eon Ticket events
legendaries.catching_tips: Identical mechanics to Latios. Slightly more defensively oriented. Both have access to a Latiasite/Latiosite in later games but in Emerald you'll just want them for Soul Dew (held item that boosts their Sp. Atk and Sp. Def).
legendaries.recommended_ball: Master Ball or Net Ball after Mean Look
legendaries.save_routine: Same as Latios - save before answering Mom. IVs/Nature lock at the news event.
legendaries.talked_fled_note: Identical to Latios. Track on the PokeNav map; Repel-bike between routes.
legendaries.repel_trick: Repel + Lv 41+ lead = guaranteed Lati-only encounter on the route currently showing it.

legendaries.name: Regirock
legendaries.location: Desert Ruins (Route 111, behind Mirage Tower area)
access_requirements: Catch a Relicanth and a Wailord, place them as the head/tail of your party
Read the Braille in Sealed Chamber (under Route 134 dive spot) - it tells you to 'go straight then right'
In the Desert Ruins chamber, walk 2 steps down from the center, then 2 right, then use Rock Smash
legendaries.catching_tips: Has Curse + Superpower + Explosion. Don't let it Explode - it WILL kill itself. Bring Pokemon with Damp ability (Poliwhirl/Wooper) to block Explosion. Use Sleep status; chip with Special Attackers since its Defense is 200 base.
legendaries.save_routine: Save outside the Desert Ruins. Each encounter requires resetting the Sealed Chamber prerequisites only ONCE - the Regis are individual saves.
legendaries.ohko_warning: Explosion at low HP is the run-killer. Damp ability or just keep it above 50% until ready.

legendaries.name: Regice
access_requirements: Same Relicanth/Wailord party requirement
Inside the cave, stand in the center for 2 minutes WITHOUT MOVING
(Some sources say walk in a counter-clockwise lap; the proven method is just stand still for 2 minutes)
legendaries.catching_tips: Massive Special Defense (200 base). Use physical attackers to chip it down - Hariyama with Brick Break, or a high-attack Steel-type. Has Explosion - use Damp Pokemon. Avoid Special moves; you'll do pitiful damage even with super effective.
legendaries.save_routine: Save before the 2-minute stand-still ritual. If you misclick a step, soft-reset is faster than re-walking.
legendaries.ohko_warning: Explosion + 200 SpDef means it rarely dies to your chip moves. Physical attackers preferred.

legendaries.name: Registeel
access_requirements: Same Relicanth/Wailord party requirement
Use Fly to access Route 120, walk to the small tomb
Inside, use Flash in the center of the room
legendaries.catching_tips: Balanced 200/200 defenses. Status it (paralyze, then put to sleep) and chip with Fighting or Fire moves. Watch for Curse (slows it but raises attack/defense) and Explosion. Steel typing resists everything except Fire, Fighting, and Ground.
legendaries.save_routine: Save before using Flash inside Ancient Tomb. Encounter is one-shot until you flee/KO.
legendaries.ohko_warning: Curse + Iron Defense + Explosion - status it FAST or you're in for a 30-turn slugfest.

legendaries.name: Mew (event only)
legendaries.location: Faraway Island (event-only, requires the Old Sea Map item from a Nintendo distribution event in 2006)
legendaries.access_requirements: Old Sea Map distribution item; not legitimately obtainable today without trade or hacking
legendaries.catching_tips: Mew runs through tall grass on Faraway Island and you have to track it without it spotting you (Solid Snake style). Once cornered, it teleports. After enough loops, you battle it at Lv 30. Use False Swipe and a Quick Ball.
legendaries.recommended_ball: Quick Ball (turn 1) - in Gen 3 use Net Ball or Ultra Ball as Quick Ball doesn't exist
legendaries.save_routine: Save before each grass-track loop on Faraway Island. Mew teleports if it sees you.
legendaries.ohko_warning: Mew's Transform copies your lead - bring a False-Swiper that can't OHKO itself.

legendaries.name: Deoxys (event only)
legendaries.access_requirements: Aurora Ticket distribution event; involves a triangle puzzle on the island
legendaries.catching_tips: In Emerald, Deoxys appears in its Speed Forme by default (different from FireRed/LeafGreen). Has Extreme Speed. Save before the puzzle - solving it spawns Deoxys and you only get one shot. Use False Swipe and Ultra Balls.
legendaries.save_routine: Save BEFORE the triangle puzzle. Solving spawns Deoxys instantly; one shot per save.
legendaries.ohko_warning: Speed Forme out-speeds nearly everything in the cartridge. False Swipe with a Quick Claw lead helps.

legendaries.name: Jirachi (event only)
legendaries.location: Bonus Disc (Pokemon Colosseum / Channel) - GameCube-to-GBA transfer event
legendaries.access_requirements: Required GameCube + Pokemon Channel or Pokemon Colosseum Bonus Disc + a link cable. Distributed 2003-04 only. Not legitimately available on stock cartridges today.
legendaries.catching_tips: Comes in a Cherish Ball at Lv 5 from the Bonus Disc - not a wild encounter. No catching minigame. Has Wish + Doom Desire as event-exclusive moves.
legendaries.recommended_ball: N/A - arrives pre-caught from the Bonus Disc
legendaries.save_routine: N/A - one-shot transfer from GameCube. Treat the Bonus Disc save as the source of truth.

hidden_secrets.title: Mirage Island
hidden_secrets.description: A tiny island that only appears off Route 130 if your party includes a Pokemon whose Personality Value matches the day's hidden randomly-generated PV (16 bits, so roughly 1-in-65,536 chance per day). The old man on Pacifidlog Town's leftmost house tells you whether it's there today. The only thing on the island is a wild Wynaut horde and a Liechi Berry. It's the rarest non-event location in the game.
hidden_secrets.tip: Build a team with as many Pokemon as possible cycled through to maximize chances. There's no way to game it - just check daily.

hidden_secrets.title: Trick House
hidden_secrets.description: Route 110, owned by the Trick Master. Each gym you beat unlocks a new puzzle floor. Rewards include TMs (TM12 Taunt, TM31 Brick Break, TM32 Double Team), the Hard Stone, and rare items. The first puzzle (a scroll-burning maze) rewards the Hard Stone.
hidden_secrets.tip: Re-enter after each badge - the Trick Master rebuilds the house with a new puzzle. The 8th puzzle gives a Magnet.

hidden_secrets.title: Berry Master's Wife (Route 123)
hidden_secrets.description: The Berry Master's wife gives you a rare berry each day if you tell her a phrase. Saying specific phrases gives specific rare berries:
hidden_secrets.tip: These rare berries can be planted and grown into infinite supplies - they're used to make max-power Pokeblocks for Contests.

hidden_secrets.title: Mossdeep Space Center NPCs
hidden_secrets.description: Talk to all the NPCs on the second floor of the Space Center. Once Team Magma's plot wraps, talk to the man in the upper-left desk - he gives you the Sun Stone if you've talked to enough scientists. The space center is also the only place in Emerald with NPC dialogue referencing Jirachi's myth.
hidden_secrets.tip: After the main story, the launch goes successfully and you can watch a rocket take off.

hidden_secrets.title: Lilycove Department Store Rooftop Lottery
hidden_secrets.description: On the rooftop of Lilycove Dept Store, there's a free vending sale during certain in-game events. Also: the Pokemon Lottery in the Pokemon Center checks all Pokemon in your PC for matching ID numbers. Trade with friends to multiply your odds - matching all 5 digits wins a Master Ball!
hidden_secrets.tip: Match 5 digits = Master Ball, 4 = PP Up, 3 = Exp Share, 2 = PP Up, 1 = nothing. Trade often.

hidden_secrets.title: Battle Frontier Symbols (post-game)
hidden_secrets.description: The Battle Frontier on the artificial island past Lilycove has 7 facilities, each with a Frontier Brain who gives a Symbol when defeated. Earning all 14 Symbols (Silver + Gold for each facility) is the true post-game completion. Frontier Brains: Anabel (Tower), Greta (Arena), Tucker (Dome), Lucy (Pike), Spenser (Palace), Brandon (Pyramid), Noland (Factory).
hidden_secrets.tip: Save before each Brain fight - you only get one shot per streak. Held items don't carry between Pyramid floors.

hidden_secrets.title: Sealed Chamber and Regi Trio
hidden_secrets.description: Under Route 134's dive spot, there's a chamber with Braille on the walls. You need a Wailord (Pokeball head of party) and a Relicanth (tail) to read the script. This unlocks the three Regi caves around Hoenn. The Braille on the walls is real Braille and translates to the actual instructions in English.
hidden_secrets.tip: Catch Relicanth via Dive on Route 124/126 - it's rare. Wailmer evolves into Wailord at Lv 40.

hidden_secrets.title: Secret Bases
hidden_secrets.description: Use Secret Power (TM43, given by an NPC in Slateport's market) on certain trees, bushes, walls, and dirt mounds to create a Secret Base. You can decorate it with dolls, mats, posters, and tents. Mix Records with friends via Link Cable to visit each others' bases - their teams become NPC trainers you can battle once per day for free EXP.
hidden_secrets.tip: Secret Bases are the original Pokemon Camp/Joint Avenue and one of the best sources of unlimited daily EXP if you have friends with Emerald carts.

hidden_secrets.title: Trainer Hill
hidden_secrets.description: Located between Mauville and Verdanturf via the cable car, Trainer Hill is a 4-floor sequential trainer rush. You earn record times. Custom trainer hills can be downloaded via the e-Reader (Japan/US event-only). Beating the default 3-trainer rooms gives a small reward, but the real prize is the speedrun leaderboard.
hidden_secrets.tip: Use Steel/Ground/Ghost coverage to handle the random trainer mix. Stock up on Ethers.

hidden_secrets.title: Move Tutors of Hoenn
hidden_secrets.description: Several NPCs across Hoenn teach moves for free or for cost:
tutors: Slateport Battle Tent area - Counter, Mimic
Fallarbor Town - teaches Draco Meteor-equivalent moves like Mega Punch/Mega Kick
Battle Frontier - Body Slam, Double-Edge, Seismic Toss, Dream Eater, Mega Punch/Kick, etc. (cost: Battle Points)
Lilycove - the Move Reminder for Heart Scales (find Heart Scales by smashing rocks or using Item Finder on the beach)
hidden_secrets.tip: Heart Scales are key for re-learning forgotten moves. Use Pickup-ability Pokemon (Linoone, Zigzagoon) to farm them.

hidden_secrets.title: Mauville Game Corner
hidden_secrets.description: Slot machines in Mauville City. Win Coins, exchange them for TMs (TM13 Ice Beam, TM23 Iron Tail, TM24 Thunderbolt, TM30 Shadow Ball, TM35 Flamethrower) or rare Pokemon (Abra, Dratini in trade games). Slots are RNG-based but the cherry/replay strategy improves odds.
hidden_secrets.tip: TM24 Thunderbolt is one of the best moves in Gen 3 and 4000 Coins. The 1000 Coin TM35 Flamethrower is also incredible.

hidden_secrets.title: Contest Halls
hidden_secrets.description: Lilycove (Master), Verdanturf (Normal), Slateport (Super), Fallarbor (Hyper). Five contest categories: Cool, Beauty, Cute, Smart, Tough. Win all 4 ranks in a category to unlock a Pokemon Picture in your Trainer Card and earn rare ribbons. Pokeblocks (made from berries with friends or PokeNav match calls) raise contest stats.
hidden_secrets.tip: Feebas evolves into Milotic only when its Beauty stat is maxed out via blue Pokeblocks, then leveling up. This is the only way to get Milotic in vanilla Emerald.

hidden_secrets.title: Feebas Tiles
hidden_secrets.description: Feebas only spawns on 6 specific tiles in Route 119, randomly chosen each game. You'll fish (Old/Good/Super Rod) on every tile until you find one with Feebas. Once found, save the location - they're consistent until you alter your Trainer ID.
hidden_secrets.tip: Use a Super Rod and bring a lot of patience. Plant Feebas tiles can be inherited from friends' Trainer IDs in some cases.

hidden_secrets.title: Trick Master TMs
hidden_secrets.description: Inside the Trick House, the Trick Master rewards puzzle completion with TMs you can't easily get elsewhere: TM12 Taunt, TM32 Double Team, TM31 Brick Break. The Brick Break is essential for Battle Frontier Screen-spammers.
hidden_secrets.tip: Use Cut and Strength frequently inside the puzzles - many tricks involve hidden items behind cuttable trees.

hidden_secrets.title: Pacifidlog Tides
hidden_secrets.description: Pacifidlog Town's currents shift directionally every few hours. The currents push you in specific directions which can either help (toward Sky Pillar) or hinder you. Use the Mach Bike to outrun them.
hidden_secrets.tip: If you're trying to reach Sky Pillar, the morning current is the most useful direction.

hidden_secrets.title: Trick Master's 8 puzzles (full list)
hidden_secrets.description: The Trick Master rebuilds his Route 110 house after every gym you clear. Each puzzle is themed and unlocks at a specific badge count. Below: every puzzle, gating badge, layout hint, and prize.

puzzles.hint: Burn the scrolls in numbered order using a Fire-type or Flannery's TM35; each scroll opens a wall.
Push boulders to unblock the correct door sequence; one Wynaut blocks the path — talk to it twice.
Step on lit panels; lit panels become unlit on entry; map the room before stepping.
Hop onto ice tiles — you slide until you hit a wall. Plan a path that ends at each switch.
Read every signpost — the order they reference is the path through the cardboard cutouts.
Acro Bike required — bunny-hop across yellow circular tiles to advance. Keep B held in place.
The mirror copy of every room is reversed; pick the door that maps to the OPPOSITE side.
Eight back-to-back trainers; pack Ethers and a status healer. Final room hides the prize behind a Strength boulder.
puzzles.prize: TM12 again / Magnet (varies) — plus a permanent Trick Master letter mailable to Secret Bases.

hidden_secrets.tip: Cut and Strength are useful in nearly every Trick House run — trees and boulders hide bonus items in most floors. Re-enter ONCE per badge for the puzzle to refresh; if you miss the visit window the prize remains until you collect.

hidden_secrets.title: Mossdeep Space Center NPC dialogue (each gives a tip)
hidden_secrets.description: The 2F NPCs each give a Pokemon-trivia line plus a small reward chain. Talk to all of them in order before the upper-left scientist hands you the Sun Stone.

npcs.tip: Confirms the launch will succeed once Team Magma's plot wraps — explicit story flag.
Explains that effort accumulates per battle — the in-cartridge tutorial for EVs in plain language.
Mentions Hidden Power's variable type — the only NPC that explicitly hints at IV-determined moves.
References a star that vanishes during the day — the cartridge's only Jirachi reference; Jirachi was distributed via Colosseum bonus disc.
Hands over the Sun Stone after you talk to all four other scientists. Required for Sunkern → Sunflora and Gloom → Bellossom.
Only appears after the rocket cutscene — references future missions, foreshadowing Deoxys (event-distributed Aurora Ticket).

hidden_secrets.tip: Visit twice: once before Team Magma's hideout (most NPCs talk about prep), and once after launch (different dialogue, plus the Sun Stone is now claimable if you missed it).

hidden_secrets.title: Mirage Island math (PV match, simplified)
hidden_secrets.description: Mirage Island appears off Route 130 only when ANY party Pokemon's lower 16 bits of Personality Value match the day's hidden 16-bit world value. Roughly 1-in-65,536 odds per day per slot — a full party of 6 unique PVs gives the best practical odds.
math: Each Pokemon has a 32-bit PV. Only the LOWER 16 bits (PV & 0xFFFF) are checked for Mirage Island.
The game stores a hidden 16-bit value rotated daily by the RTC (battery-dependent).
If ANY party member's lower 16 bits == today's value, the island spawns.
Odds with one Pokemon: 1 / 65,536 = ~0.00153% per day.
Odds with six unique-PV Pokemon: ~6 / 65,536 ≈ 0.00916% per day — still rare but multiplicatively better.
hidden_secrets.tip: Cycle eggs through your party from breeding to constantly rotate fresh PVs. The old man on Pacifidlog's leftmost house tells you whether the island spawned today — always check him before sailing.

hidden_secrets.title: Sealed Chamber Braille translation
hidden_secrets.description: The Sealed Chamber under Route 134's dive spot uses real Braille (the Western alphabet variant). Each wall plate translates to a one-to-one English sentence. You need both Wailord (lead) and Relicanth (tail) to read certain plates.

braille_walls.action: Use Dig (TM28) on the marked dirt mound to drop into the lower chamber.
Place Relicanth at the END of your party (slot 6).
Place Wailord at the START of your party (slot 1). Lead Wailord, tail Relicanth — the surface text inverts the slot meaning.

braille_walls.braille: IF THINGS ARE IN ORDER, A NEW WORLD OF POKEMON OPENS UP
braille_walls.action: With the right party order, an earthquake unlocks Regirock (Desert Ruins, Route 111), Regice (Island Cave, Route 105), and Registeel (Ancient Tomb, Route 120).

regi_chambers.name: Regirock
regi_chambers.solution: Walk two steps right and two steps down from the entry, then use Strength.

regi_chambers.name: Regice
regi_chambers.solution: Stand on the central tile and wait ~2 real-world minutes without moving — verify timer in mGBA.

regi_chambers.name: Registeel
regi_chambers.solution: Stand on the dead-center tile of the chamber and use Fly (out of battle, from menu) to trigger the door.

hidden_secrets.tip: Wailord reaches Lv 40 to evolve from Wailmer; Relicanth requires Dive on Routes 124/126/128 — 8% encounter rate. Stockpile Ultra Balls for Relicanth.

hidden_secrets.title: Lottery (Lilycove Pokemon Center)
hidden_secrets.description: Once per real-world day, the Lottery NPC inside Lilycove's Pokemon Center compares a randomly generated 5-digit Trainer ID against every Pokemon ID in your boxes + party. Prizes scale by digit-match length, counted from the rightmost digit.

rules.frequency: Once per RTC day (resets at 00:00 local).
rules.rolling_method: 5-digit number is drawn after you talk to the NPC. Compares against every Pokemon's OT ID.
rules.match_direction: Counts consecutive matching digits starting from the RIGHTMOST digit (ones place).

hidden_secrets.tip: Trade with friends to multiply your unique OT IDs — every additional ID raises your odds. Check daily; saving before talking to the NPC is futile (the roll is committed to the save).

hidden_secrets.title: Pacifidlog daily NPC trades
hidden_secrets.description: Several boats and houses on Pacifidlog Town offer daily trades — NPCs swap a fresh Pokemon every RTC day. Useful for filling Pokedex gaps when no in-game trade exists.

trades.tip: One-time swap, but Horsea isn't in vanilla wild Emerald — worth the trade.
Skitty is wild on Route 116, but the traded one comes at a higher level — free EXP.

trades.npc: Old man on the leftmost house
trades.tip: Tells you whether Mirage Island spawned today. Free intel.

hidden_secrets.tip: Verify trade table on your save — minor trades vary by Emerald revision.

hidden_secrets.title: Verdanturf hot-spring trade
hidden_secrets.description: The southern Verdanturf NPC inside the small house near the entrance offers a one-time in-game trade: Bidoof-tier Pokemon for a Skitty (ribbon-holder).
details: Offers: Skitty (named 'Skitty' or as nicknamed by NPC)
Wants: Pokemon from the Pickup-line family — verify with Zigzagoon
Note: Verify which species the trade asks for — some Emerald revisions ask for different species. Save before the trade.
hidden_secrets.tip: Skitty's signature is Cute Charm + Attract for status disruption. The traded Skitty's nature/IVs may differ from a wild catch — inspect first.

hidden_secrets.title: Mauville move-tutor (Rollout / Fury Cutter minigame)
hidden_secrets.description: An NPC on the south side of Mauville teaches consecutive-hit moves like Rollout and Fury Cutter — free, one-shot. Both deal scaling damage if you connect every turn.

moves_taught.use: Doubles each consecutive hit (5 turns max). Pair with Defense Curl for 4× base damage.
Doubles each consecutive hit (5 turns max). Combines with Heracross / Ninjask Speed Boost for chains.

hidden_secrets.tip: Only one move per Pokemon per visit — plan recipients ahead. Re-trigger by leaving the room and re-entering only works once per save in some revisions; verify.

breeding_basics.where: Day Care on Route 117 (Mauville to Verdanturf path). Drop two compatible Pokemon (opposite genders, same Egg Group) and walk in steps. The old man outside paces - if he's facing the road, no egg yet. If he's facing the fence, egg is ready.
breeding_basics.ditto_role: Ditto can breed with any non-legendary, non-Ditto Pokemon. Trade one in from FireRed/LeafGreen Trainer Tower or Pokemon Mansion.
breeding_basics.egg_groups: Pokemon must share at least one Egg Group. Some Pokemon are in groups like 'Monster + Dragon' giving wide compatibility.
breeding_basics.everstone: If the FEMALE holds an Everstone, the offspring inherits her Nature 50% of the time. This is THE primary way to get a specific Nature in Gen 3.
inheritance: The species comes from the female (or non-Ditto if Ditto is involved)
TMs/Egg Moves: father teaches compatible TMs and special egg moves to the offspring
IVs: 3 IVs are randomly inherited from parents in Gen 3 (more in later gens). Random across HP/Atk/Def/SpA/SpD/Spe.

iv_basics.what_are_ivs: Individual Values - hidden 0-31 stats per Pokemon for each stat (HP, Atk, Def, SpA, SpD, Spe). They're randomly assigned at capture/hatch and never change. Higher IV = higher final stat.
iv_basics.checking_ivs: No in-game IV judge in Emerald. Use online IV calculators with your Pokemon's level/nature/EVs/stats to back-calculate.
iv_basics.max_iv_impact: At Lv 100, a 31 IV stat is +31 over a 0 IV stat. For HP, that's +31 HP. Significant.
iv_basics.hidden_power: The type and power of Hidden Power are determined entirely by IVs. Can range from any type and 30-70 power. Used to give coverage to Pokemon (e.g., Hidden Power Fire on a Grass-type vs Steel).

ev_basics.what_are_evs: Effort Values - earned by defeating Pokemon. Each species gives specific EV yields. Max 510 EVs total per Pokemon, max 252 per stat (rounds to 255 with no benefit past 252). Every 4 EVs in a stat = +1 to that stat at Lv 100.
ev_basics.vitamins: HP Up (HP), Protein (Atk), Iron (Def), Calcium (SpA), Zinc (SpD), Carbos (Speed) each give +10 EVs. Cap at 100 EVs per stat from vitamins; rest must come from battle.
ev_basics.ev_reset_berries: Pomeg (-HP), Kelpsy (-Atk), Qualot (-Def), Hondew (-SpA), Grepa (-SpD), Tamato (-Spe) each remove 10 EVs. Useful to fix a mistrained Pokemon.
ev_basics.macho_brace_pokerus: Macho Brace doubles EV gain (halves Speed). Pokerus (rare wild status virus) ALSO doubles EVs and stacks with Macho Brace for 4x. Pokerus spreads between adjacent party members.

ev_training_spots_emerald.hp: Marill on Route 102 (1 HP EV) or Wailmer on Route 103 (1 HP EV). For 2 HP: Wynaut hatched from Lavaridge egg.
ev_training_spots_emerald.attack: Mightyena/Poochyena on Route 120 (2 Atk). Or Vigoroth in Petalburg Woods (2 Atk).
ev_training_spots_emerald.defense: Geodude on Granite Cave/Victory Road (1 Def). Trapinch in the desert (1 Def).
ev_training_spots_emerald.special_attack: Tentacool on water routes (1 SpA), or Gulpin (1 SpA) on Route 110.
ev_training_spots_emerald.special_defense: Tentacool/Tentacruel give SpD + SpA. For pure SpD: Roselia (2 SpA actually) - cleanest pure SpD is Spheal (1 SpD).
ev_training_spots_emerald.speed: Zubat in caves (1 Spe), Magikarp from fishing (1 Spe), or Electrode from New Mauville (2 Spe).

breeding_iv_ev.tip: Emerald's EV training is grindy. Always equip Macho Brace, infect your team with Pokerus, and head to a known EV spot. Use Repels matched to the species' level so you only encounter one Pokemon type.

egg_step_counts.overview: Eggs in Gen 3 hatch after a fixed number of overworld steps. Each species has an 'egg cycle' value; one cycle = 256 steps. Halved when a party member has Magma Armor or Flame Body.

magma_armor: Camerupt — does NOT have it (has Magma Armor only on Numel pre-evo, verify in-game)
fastest_hatch_abilities.stack_rule: ONE party member with the ability halves cycles; STACKING multiple does NOT compound. Just pack one.

common_step_counts.species: Beldum (CANNOT BE BRED — single-stage gift, no eggs)

common_step_counts.comment: Legendaries you'd 'expect' egg counts for (Latios, Latias, Rayquaza, Mew, etc.) ARE NOT BREEDABLE in Gen 3 — they have no Egg Group.

fastest_hatch_setup.step_1: Catch a Slugma on Route 113 (Magma Armor; ~5% rate; common enough).
fastest_hatch_setup.step_2: Place Slugma in your party in any slot — it doesn't need to be the lead.
fastest_hatch_setup.step_3: Mount the Mach Bike on a long, flat route (Route 119 or Cycling Road) and ride back-and-forth. Each tile = 1 step.
fastest_hatch_setup.step_4: Hatch a Magcargo-cycle baby (5120 → 2560 steps) in roughly 5 minutes of biking.

egg_step_counts.tip: Eggs ONLY count overworld steps — battles do not advance the timer. Save state-skipping in emulators speeds this up massively.

light_ball_clarification.fact: Light Ball held by Pikachu boosts the Pikachu's Special Attack and Attack in battle (2x).
light_ball_clarification.volt_tackle_note: Volt Tackle as Pichu's egg move (when mother Pikachu/Raichu holds Light Ball) is a GEN 4+ mechanic. In Emerald, holding Light Ball does NOT give Pichu Volt Tackle — Pichu doesn't even learn Volt Tackle in Gen 3. Volt Tackle didn't exist yet.
light_ball_clarification.tip: If you transfer Pichu to Gen 4 (Pal Park), THEN breed in Gen 4 with Light Ball, you can get Volt Tackle. Not in Emerald.

power_items_absent.fact: Power Bracer, Power Belt, Power Anklet, Power Lens, Power Band, and Power Weight are all GEN 4 items. They guarantee a single inherited IV from the holder.
power_items_absent.implication_for_emerald: Gen 3 IV breeding is pure RNG: 3 random IVs inherited at random from the parents, 3 fresh rolls. Macho Brace exists but only doubles EV gain — does NOT pass IVs.
power_items_absent.destiny_knot_note: Destiny Knot exists as a MOVE-blocking item in Gen 3 (one move blocks both sides; flavor item) but NOT as the IV-inheritance item that boosts to 5 inherited IVs (Gen 6+).

egg_moves_chains.overview: An egg move (a move not in the species' level-up pool) passes from the FATHER if he knows it AND the species can legitimately learn it. Chains let you stack multiple egg moves on one offspring through generations of breeding.
egg_moves_chains.rule: Father's TM moves pass IF the offspring can learn the same TM. Father's egg moves pass IF the offspring's species lists them.
chain_example_belly_drum_marill: Step 1: Need Belly Drum on a Marill. Marill's egg-move list includes Belly Drum.
Step 2: Belly Drum is a Polywag-line egg move. Catch a male Polywag/Polywhirl that knows Belly Drum (or breed one).
Step 3: Polywhirl shares the Water 1 egg group with Marill.
Step 4: Breed male Polywhirl (Belly Drum) × female Marill — egg hatches as Marill knowing Belly Drum.
Bonus: Female Marill can ALSO hold Everstone for nature-locking, so the offspring inherits Mom's nature 50% of the time AND Belly Drum from Dad.
chain_example_dragon_dance_bagon: Step 1: Bagon's egg-move list includes Dragon Dance.
Step 2: Dragon Dance is learned naturally by Horsea/Seadra. Horsea/Seadra share Dragon egg group with Bagon.
Step 3: Catch male Horsea/Seadra. Level to learn Dragon Dance (Lv 35 Seadra).
Step 4: Breed male Seadra (DD) × female Bagon — egg = Bagon with Dragon Dance.
egg_moves_chains.tip: Chains are limited by egg-group compatibility. Cross-reference Bulbapedia's Gen 3 egg-move tables before committing — some chains are 4+ generations long.

day_care_man_xp_tracking.fact: The Day Care Man on Route 117 tracks how many levels each Pokemon has gained while in the Day Care. He charges 100 P per level when you withdraw.
level_up_logic: Levels grow proportionally to overworld steps you take (NOT battles).

Pokemon do NOT learn level-up moves while in the Day Care that would replace existing moves — older moves are forgotten WITHOUT prompt as new ones are auto-added at the cost of the oldest. Pull a Pokemon out before key level-ups if you want to keep specific moves.
Pokemon at Lv 100 cost 100 P to withdraw (no level gain) — check the man if you're unsure.
day_care_man_xp_tracking.egg_unrelated_to_levels: The egg counter and the level counter are independent. Stash a baby in the Day Care, withdraw the egg, and you'll pay only the level fee for the parent — the egg is free.
day_care_man_xp_tracking.tip: If you're not actively breeding, withdraw and re-deposit periodically to check the Day Care man's outside-pacing direction (if facing fence, an egg is ready).

tips: Choose Mudkip if it's your first Emerald playthrough - easiest run by far
Do the bag-grab tutorial in Petalburg Woods - free Potions
Catch a Wurmple on Route 101 (free Bug coverage; evolves into Beautifly or Dustox at random)
Talk to your dad in Petalburg Gym - he gives you HM01-related dialogue but the badge comes later
Catch Shroomish in Petalburg Woods - it evolves into Breloom and learns Mach Punch (priority Fighting move) at Lv 23. Insane against Roxanne and Norman.
Battle every trainer for EXP - Emerald's EXP curve is steep early
Buy 5-10 Potions and 3-5 Antidotes before Petalburg Woods (Poison Sting Wurmples are everywhere)
Save your money - Poke Balls cost 200 each but the Devon Goods sidequest gives you 5 free Premier Balls
Catch Aron in Granite Cave (1F) - it evolves into Lairon (Lv 32) then Aggron (Lv 42). Massive late-game Steel/Rock wall.
Talk to Steven in the deepest part of Granite Cave - he gives you TM47 Steel Wing for the Devon Goods. Steven is the OG champion (Wallace took over for Emerald) and is foreshadowed throughout.
Don't waste Repels in Granite Cave - the Aron encounter rate is decent and you want it
Sail back to Slateport to deliver the Devon Goods - this opens up Cycling Road

Get the Mach Bike or Acro Bike at Rydel's Cycles in Mauville. Mach Bike is needed for Mirage Tower (Anorith/Lileep) and Sky Pillar; Acro Bike for some Secret Base spots and Trick House. You can swap anytime by returning to Rydel.
Catch Electrike on Route 110 - evolves into Manectric, one of the best Electric types in Emerald
Slot machine the Mauville Game Corner if you're patient - TM24 Thunderbolt for 4000 Coins is a game-changer
Wattson's Dynamo Badge unlocks Rock Smash - go back and Rock Smash boulders for items, Geodude, and Nosepass

Mirage Tower in the desert (need Mach Bike + Go-Goggles) - choose ONE: Root Fossil (Lileep/Cradily) or Claw Fossil (Anorith/Armaldo). The other crumbles into the sand forever. Pick Anorith if you're light on a Rock-type, Lileep if you want a tanky Grass/Rock.
After climbing Mt. Chimney, you fight Team Magma/Aqua at the summit - heal first
Lavaridge gives you a free Wynaut egg from the old woman near the hot springs - Wobbuffet has utility in doubles
Always use the Hot Springs - they fully heal your team for free (faster than Pokemon Center for grinders)

at_town_littleroot: Pick up Running Shoes from Mom
PokeNav not yet — Birch's lab tutorial only
money_tips: Sell nothing yet — keep everything for Petalburg buy round
missables: Wurmple (only at Route 101 Lv 2-3 — easy to skip past)

at_town_petalburg: Talk to Dad in gym (HM01 setup dialogue)
at_town_rustboro: Devon Corp 3F — get the Devon Goods quest
Trainer School NPCs give 1 free Potion each (~5 total)
money_tips: Sell 1-2 Pickup-grabbed items (Potion, Antidote drops sum to ~600 P)
DON'T sell Nuggets if Pickup grabs one — 5,000 P each in Mauville

at_town_dewford: Mr. Briney's house — first sail unlocks
Hidden Silk Scarf in the sand (Itemfinder)
Talk to Steven at the deepest part of Granite Cave for TM47 Steel Wing
money_tips: Sell Heart Scales picked up via Rock Smash for ~250 P each only if cash-strapped — they're better used at Lilycove Move Reminder
missables: Mawile / Sableye on Granite Cave depths (rare)

at_town_mauville: Rydel's Cycles — pick Mach or Acro (free swaps)
Game Corner: TM30 Shadow Ball (1500 coins is the cheap one)
Wattson rematch quest: New Mauville generator → free TM24 Thunderbolt
tm_acquisitions_in_phase: TM09 Bullet Seed — Cycling Road hidden (Itemfinder)
TM24 Thunderbolt (Game Corner 4000 OR free post-quest)
money_tips: Sell Big Pearl if Pickup grabs one (3,750 P)
Sell Stardust (1,000 P) only if cash-strapped — stack 5+ first
Cycling Road has hidden items + free trainer EXP one-way
missables: Plusle / Minun (Route 110 — pair them in doubles)
Trick House Puzzle 3 reward Smoke Ball (re-enter after Wattson)

at_town_verdanturf: Wanda's husband — Rusturf Tunnel post-clear gives Mental Herb
TM45 Attract usable here for status disruption
at_town_lavaridge: Hot Springs — free heals + Wynaut egg from old woman
Herb shop — bitter healing items lower friendship; AVOID for Crobat plans
tm_acquisitions_in_phase: TM31 Brick Break — Trick House Puzzle 6 (post-Winona, but still relevant for Aerial Ace setup)
TM43 Secret Power — Slateport Market gift after Norman (slightly later)
money_tips: Lava Cookie at Lavaridge stand — cures all status (free at the old woman)
Sell Stardust here — Mauville store doesn't take it; Lavaridge store does
missables: Wynaut egg (Lavaridge old woman — one-time gift)

first_wave_must_catches.missable_window: Rare — 4% encounter rate; only spawns when route's weather is set to rain (Route 119 weather, RTC-dependent). Verify rain before farming.
Once you progress past the woods to Rustboro, you can return — but if you sail to Dewford without going back, you skip a Hoenn-dex slot.
Single-route in early game; Beldum/Bagon are post-game alternatives but Breloom carries you through gyms 1, 5, and Frontier.
Available ONLY after defeating the Champion. Check Steven's house for the Pokeball on the desk.
Late-game catch. 5% encounter rate. Required for Salamence.
TID-locked. Must fish each tile until you find one. Once found, mark on a map.

daily_npc_retrade_locations.trade: Horsea for Bagon (one-time, but a daily flag exists in some revisions)
Daily phrase → rare berry — see Hidden Secrets phrases
Daily Energy Powder / Heal Powder swap (verify revision)

pokemon_tier_list.criteria: Rankings prioritize main-story efficiency: how cleanly each Pokemon handles Hoenn's eight gyms, the rivals, the Magma/Aqua plot, and the Sidney/Phoebe/Glacia/Drake/Wallace E4 ladder. We weight (1) availability timing relative to level cap, (2) movepool depth without TM hoarding, (3) gym/E4 matchup coverage, and (4) time-investment-to-payoff. We DO NOT rank by competitive Smogon ladder performance — Frontier viability is a tiebreaker, not the headline.

S.name: Swampert
S.justification: Mud Shot one-shots Wattson, Earthquake/Ice Beam erase Drake's Salamence and Flygon, and the 4x Grass weakness is a non-issue against Wallace. Solos five of eight gyms with no babying.

S.name: Sceptile
S.justification: Base 120 Speed outpaces every E4 lead. Leaf Blade two-shots Wallace's Whiscash and Milotic; Crunch lets it pierce Tate & Liza without dying to Psychic.

S.name: Blaziken
S.justification: Double Kick at Lv 16 carries Roxanne; Sky Uppercut at Lv 36 cleaves Norman's Slaking and Glacia's Walrein. Blaze Kick + Bulk Up is the cleanest Phoebe answer the starter trio offers.

S.name: Salamence
S.availability: Bagon @ Meteor Falls B1F (post-7th badge, Lv 25 surf area)
S.justification: 135 Attack with Dragon Claw + Earthquake + Aerial Ace + Crunch hits the entire E4 super-effectively somewhere. Intimidate softens physical hits long enough to set up a single Dragon Dance and end the run.

S.name: Metagross
S.availability: Beldum gift from Steven (post-game, Steven's house in Mossdeep)
S.justification: 70/130/95 bulk eats Sidney's Dark moves while Meteor Mash two-shots them, and Earthquake dismantles Magma/Aqua leaders. Post-game only, but remains the single best Frontier mon you can build.

S.name: Manectric
S.justification: Available before Wattson, who he then trivializes via Quick Attack + Howl. Thunderbolt OHKOes Winona's Pelipper and Skarmory and 2HKOs Wallace's Milotic; Intimidate keeps Sharpedo and Crawdaunt out of Crunch range.

S.name: Slaking
S.niche: 670 BST brick that loafs every other turn.
S.justification: Truant is the price for a 160 Attack Earthquake that two-shots most E4 leads. Schedule Hyper Potions on the loaf turn and it functionally trades 1-for-1 with anything Glacia, Phoebe, or Sidney bring.

A.name: Gardevoir
A.niche: Calm Mind sweeper with full coverage tutors.
A.justification: Calm Mind + Psychic boosts past Phoebe's Dusclops, and Thunderbolt/Ice Punch tutors round it out for Wallace and Drake. Trace ability copies Levitate against Claydol in Tate & Liza.

A.name: Aggron
A.niche: Physical wall with Rock Head Double-Edge.
A.justification: Rock Head zeroes Double-Edge recoil for spam-killing Winona's birds, and 180 base Defense soaks Drake's Dragon Claws. Earthquake patches the 4x Fighting/Ground hole for the rest of the team.

A.name: Flygon
A.justification: Levitate gives a free switch on Wattson and Norman's Earthquake, while Dragon Claw + Earthquake + Rock Slide hits 14 of 17 types neutrally or better. Crunch from tutor cleans up Tate & Liza without tanking Psychic.

A.name: Walrein
A.niche: Thick Fat Ice tank that walls Drake.
A.justification: Thick Fat halves Fire damage so Glacia's Walrein mirror-match goes your way; Surf + Ice Beam two-shots all four of Drake's dragons. Body Slam paralysis keeps Salamence in check after Intimidate fades.

A.name: Hariyama
A.niche: Massive HP brick with Thick Fat.
A.justification: 144 base HP plus Thick Fat means Glacia's Ice moves chip rather than KO, and Vital Throw never misses Sidney's Cacturne. Belly Drum at full HP one-shots most non-Ghost E4 leads.

A.name: Heracross
A.justification: Megahorn at 120 BP is the single hardest hit you can land on Phoebe's Dusclops short of a Crit. Guts + a Flame Orb turns Brick Break into a 270 BP nuke for Sidney's whole team.

A.name: Crobat
A.justification: 130 Speed outruns Winona's Altaria and Drake's Salamence. Cross Poison + Aerial Ace handles every Grass and Fighting type the game throws, and Mean Look + Fly is the standard catch tool for the Latios/Latias roamer.

A.name: Magneton
A.justification: 11 resistances and a Steel immunity to Poison let it wall Winona's Skarmory while Thunderbolt OHKOs it back. Two-shots every member of Wallace's gym except Whiscash, who eats a switch into Flygon.

A.name: Breloom
A.justification: Spore is a 100% accuracy sleep — opens any matchup, including Norman's Slaking and Sidney's Absol. Sky Uppercut + Mach Punch + Giga Drain cover Rock, Dark, and Water without items.

A.name: Linoone
A.niche: Pickup utility plus Belly Drum + ExtremeSpeed.
A.justification: Pickup ability nets Nuggets, Rare Candies, and Full Restores across a playthrough. Belly Drum + ExtremeSpeed (TM/breeding) is a legitimate cleanup option once the Frontier opens; otherwise it carries Cut, Surf, Strength, Rock Smash, and Dig.

A.name: Swellow
A.justification: Aerial Ace at Lv 13 trivializes Brawly. Held Toxic Orb + Guts + Facade is a 140 BP STAB that two-shots most of Phoebe's Banettes and the bulk of Drake's team after Intimidate-free entries.

A.name: Skarmory
A.justification: 140 Defense plus 9 resistances means Sidney's Mightyena and Cacturne plink for nothing while Steel Wing + Drill Peck chip back. Spikes flips Phoebe's bulky Dusclops switch-ins into 2HKOs.

A.name: Camerupt
A.justification: Solid Rock halves the 4x Water hit just enough to survive a Surf and OHKO back with Earthquake. Eruption at full HP melts Phoebe and most of Sidney; switch out before any Hydro Pump.

A.name: Tentacruel
A.niche: Bulky special Water with Liquid Ooze.
A.justification: Liquid Ooze punishes Sidney's Cacturne Giga Drain. Sludge Bomb + Ice Beam + Surf is a clean Drake answer, and 120 Sp.Def shrugs off Wallace's special threats.

A.name: Gyarados
A.availability: Magikarp @ Route 119 fishing (Old Rod) or Old Man trade
A.justification: Intimidate + 125 Attack means a single Dragon Dance turns Earthquake/Bite/Surf into one-shot fodder against Drake. Magikarp grind is brutal but no other physical Water hits this hard pre-Frontier.

A.name: Donphan
A.justification: 120 base Attack and Defense let it eat a Thunderbolt from Wattson before flattening him with Magnitude. Rapid Spin clears Spikes for the team during Frontier streaks.

A.name: Sharpedo
A.justification: Rough Skin chips contact attackers and 95 Speed lets it Crunch Tate & Liza's Lunatone before Calm Mind ever lands. Frail enough to fold to a stiff breeze, but it earns the slot in any rush comp.

A.name: Kingdra
A.justification: The only Dragon in Hoenn with no 4x weakness — Ice does neutral damage. Hydro Pump + Dragon Breath is a clean Drake mirror, and Sleep Talk gives a Frontier-grade safety net.

A.name: Glalie
A.availability: Snorunt @ Shoal Cave inner ice room (post-Winona)
A.justification: Inner Focus blocks Hypnosis from Tate & Liza's Lunatone. Ice Beam two-shots Drake; Self-Destruct from a Choice-less attacker still cleanly removes any single Phoebe/Glacia ace.

A.name: Cradily
A.availability: Lileep fossil @ Mirage Tower (Claw Fossil pick) — one-time
A.niche: Suction Cup wall with Ingrain + Giga Drain.
A.justification: Six resistances including Wallace's STAB Water (with Recover support via Ingrain). Giga Drain + Ancient Power covers Wallace, Glacia's Sealeo, and Drake's Flygon in one moveset.

A.name: Armaldo
A.availability: Anorith fossil @ Mirage Tower (Root Fossil pick) — one-time
A.justification: Battle Armor blocks crits from Sidney's Slash spam and Phoebe's Banette Pursuit. Rock Slide + Brick Break + Ancient Power is one of the cleanest Sidney sweeps available pre-Frontier.

B.name: Ludicolo
B.availability: Lotad @ Route 102 (4% encounter); needs Water Stone
B.justification: After the Water Stone (sold at Mossdeep), Ludicolo only fears Bug/Flying/Poison. Giga Drain + Ice Beam handles Wallace and Drake; Leech Seed + Substitute is a legitimate Frontier set.

B.name: Shiftry
B.availability: Seedot @ Route 102 (4% encounter); needs Leaf Stone
B.justification: Early Bullet Seed + Faint Attack covers gym 1 and is the only mid-tier Dark with Explosion. Falls off without a Leaf Stone (purchasable post-Wallace) and slow Speed limits Frontier viability.

B.name: Pelipper
B.niche: Cheap early Surfer with Roost-tier longevity.
B.justification: Carries Surf and Fly at the same time, freeing two team slots. Ice Beam + Surf + Fly is enough to limp through Wallace and Drake's Flygon, but offensive stats are mediocre.

B.name: Roselia
B.niche: Special tank with Toxic + Giga Drain.
B.justification: Natural Cure clears burns/sleep on switch. Synthesis + Giga Drain self-heals through Wallace's Whiscash, but stays Roselia in Gen 3 (Roserade requires later-gen Shiny Stone).

B.name: Xatu
B.justification: Synchronize transfers natures for breeding, and Psychic + Steel Wing covers most B-tier matchups. Outclassed offensively by Gardevoir but fills the early Flying-Psychic dual-coverage slot.

B.name: Claydol
B.justification: Immune to Wattson and Norman's Earthquake spam. Rapid Spin + Light Screen + Reflect makes it a Frontier doubles staple, but mediocre 70/70 offenses cap its solo carry.

B.name: Whiscash
B.justification: Same dual STAB as Swampert with Amnesia for boosting against Wallace. Quad Grass weakness and lower bulk relegate it to B; works well as a Swampert understudy if your starter was Treecko or Torchic.

B.name: Crawdaunt
B.justification: Hyper Cutter blocks Intimidate, so it goes toe-to-toe with Sharpedo. Slow Speed (55) is the killer — it always trades, never sweeps.

B.name: Banette
B.justification: Insomnia blocks Lunatone's Hypnosis at Tate & Liza. Will-O-Wisp + Shadow Ball cripples physical attackers, but 64 Speed and zero recovery keep it out of A.

B.name: Dusclops
B.niche: Pure wall with Will-O-Wisp + Pain Split.
B.justification: 135/135 Defense/SpDef. Will-O-Wisp + Pain Split is the textbook Frontier stall set. No reliable recovery and pitiful offense — a true wall, nothing more.

B.name: Plusle
B.justification: Helping Hand + Encore + Thunder Wave is the cleanest doubles assist Hoenn offers, especially for Tate & Liza prep. Solo offense is too low for singles main story.

B.name: Minun
B.justification: Higher base SpDef than Plusle; identical role. Pair with a heavy hitter in the Battle Tent doubles for free wins.

B.name: Volbeat
B.niche: Tail Glow + Baton Pass setup mon.
B.justification: Tail Glow is a +3 Sp.Atk boost — Baton Pass it to Manectric or Gardevoir for an instant sweep. Gimmick, but the only legal Tail Glow user pre-Frontier.

B.name: Illumise
B.justification: Encore traps setup moves (looking at you, Bulk Up Slaking). Pairs with Volbeat for the only legal +3 SpAtk Baton Pass chain in Hoenn.

B.name: Beautifly
B.justification: Silver Wind has a 10% chance to boost every stat — practical setup over a 5-turn Frontier streak. Acceptable Brawly answer, irrelevant by mid-game.

B.name: Dustox
B.justification: Shield Dust ignores secondary effects. Toxic + Light Screen + Whirlwind shuffles set-up sweepers, but offense is nonexistent.

B.name: Spinda
B.justification: Own Tempo + Teeter Dance flips a doubles match upside-down. Each Spinda has a unique sprite pattern; offensive ceiling is low but the Frontier doubles utility is real.

B.name: Chimecho
B.justification: Heal Bell + Psychic + Calm Mind makes it the one Frontier cleric you can catch. Encounter rate is the only reason it's not A-tier; bring Sweet Scent and a Repel.

B.name: Mawile
B.justification: Intimidate + Iron Defense + Baton Pass is a Frontier doubles staple. Iron Head from move tutor lands flinches, but 50/85 offenses cap it solo.

B.name: Lunatone
B.justification: Calm Mind + Psychic + Ice Beam handles Drake's Flygon and Salamence in one set. Available right after Brawly and stays serviceable through E4 if leveled.

B.name: Solrock
B.justification: Levitate + four resistances. SolarBeam under sun handles Wallace's Whiscash; underwhelming Speed and weak Special movepool keep it out of A.

B.name: Sableye
B.availability: Granite Cave B1F (Sapphire/Emerald), Smeltery / Rusturf for Ruby trade — natively in Emerald
B.justification: Pre-Fairy era, Dark/Ghost has only one weakness: nothing. Recover + Knock Off + Shadow Ball is a Frontier stall package; low stats limit single-mon dominance.

C.name: Cacturne
C.justification: Sand Veil in the desert turns it into an evasion menace. Spikes + Needle Arm + Faint Attack is workable, but 55 Speed and middling bulk make it a B-minus pick.

C.name: Vibrava
C.niche: Mid-evo with Levitate, no real STAB until Lv 45.
C.justification: Decent enough to hold the slot until Lv 45 Flygon. Don't fight Drake with Vibrava — wait the ten levels, the spike is real.

C.name: Wailord
C.justification: 170 HP soaks Drake's Dragon Claws long enough to set up Water Spout. Outclassed by Walrein and Sharpedo for offensive Water duty, but matchless as a Frontier mixed wall.

C.name: Castform
C.justification: Changes type with weather. Useful in doubles with a Sunny Day partner; outright bad in singles. Take it for the Pokedex slot.

C.name: Kecleon
C.justification: Color Change resists the move it just took, so Sidney's Knock Off + Slash combo never lands a super-effective hit. Slow as molasses; pure novelty pick.

C.name: Absol
C.justification: Super Luck + Razor Claw (Frontier prize) push crit rate to 50%. Swords Dance + Aerial Ace one-shots Phoebe's whole team; frailness keeps it from A.

C.name: Zangoose
C.justification: Immunity prevents poison, so Toxic Orb skips it — Facade alone needs another item. Slash + Crush Claw is a passable physical Sidney check, but lacks a true 6th-slot win condition.

C.name: Seviper
C.availability: Route 114 grass (Sapphire exclusive — trade-only in Emerald)
C.justification: Glare paralyzes Ground types and Crunch handles Phoebe. Trade-locked in Emerald; bring it only if you want type variety.

C.name: Beldum
C.niche: Patience-tax mon — only knows Take Down + Hold Back until Lv 20.
C.justification: Pre-Metang stage is terrible. Listed only as a bridge note: grind it to Lv 45 and it becomes the S-tier Metagross — until then, bench it.

C.name: Bagon
C.justification: Same trap as Beldum: a 600-BST payoff at Lv 50. Grind in Meteor Falls Lv 35-40 wild battles or bring an Exp Share.

C.name: Numel
C.justification: Listed because the Numel-to-Camerupt jump at Lv 33 is enormous; treat the pre-evo as a setup phase rather than a battler.

honorable_mentions.name: Altaria
honorable_mentions.justification: Sing + Dragon Dance + Earthquake + Aerial Ace is a fine alternative to Salamence if you want a Hoenn-native Dragon ten levels earlier. Loses out only because Salamence outclasses it after Lv 50.

honorable_mentions.name: Milotic
honorable_mentions.availability: Feebas @ Route 119 specific tiles (or Beauty contest evo)
honorable_mentions.justification: Marvel Scale boosts Defense by 1.5x when statused. Recover + Ice Beam + Surf + Toxic is a true Frontier-tier set. Listed in HM only because the catch process is brutal — six tiles on Route 119 with a 50% encounter rate.

honorable_mentions.name: Lairon
honorable_mentions.availability: Aron evo @ Lv 32 — Granite Cave / Iron Island
honorable_mentions.justification: Strong enough to anchor against Norman and Winona before the Lv 42 Aggron evolution. Use as a holdover — don't expect more.

honorable_mentions.name: Vigoroth
honorable_mentions.justification: Hyper-active intermediate before Slaking. Faint Attack + Slash + Bulk Up keeps it real until Lv 36 Slaking — at which point Truant returns and you swap roles.

honorable_mentions.name: Wynaut
honorable_mentions.justification: Shadow Tag + Counter/Mirror Coat is a Frontier favourite for trapping single-target attackers. Singles main story role is essentially zero.

honorable_mentions.name: Tropius
honorable_mentions.niche: Listed again here as a 4-of-a-kind HM slave (Cut/Fly/Strength/Flash).
honorable_mentions.justification: The only Pokemon in Hoenn that can legally hold Cut, Fly, Strength, AND Flash simultaneously. Frees four party slots.

offensive.tagline: Six attackers, no fillers. Built to sweep Hoenn before anyone gets to move twice.

defensive.tagline: Walls, Recover, Toxic, switch. Made for Frontier streaks; main story works fine too.

hm_compatibility._meta: HM compatibility — which Pokemon can learn each HM (rough guide for Hoenn)
HM01_Cut: Most Grass / Bug starters and mid-line; Hoenn HM slaves: Zigzagoon/Linoone, Tropius, Treecko line, Slakoth line
HM02_Fly: Most pure Flying types: Swellow (Taillow), Pelipper (Wingull), Altaria (Swablu), Crobat (Zubat). Skarmory yes.
HM03_Surf: All Water types: Marshtomp/Swampert, Wailmer/Wailord, Tentacruel, Walrein, Milotic, Sharpedo. Linoone can learn Surf via HM (HM compat is broad in Gen 3).
HM04_Strength: Most physical Pokemon: Linoone, Hariyama, Slaking, Aggron, Sharpedo, even Salamence. Wide compat.
HM05_Flash: Most Pokemon — but mediocre 70-acc Normal status move. Use HM-slave only.
HM06_Rock_Smash: Most physical Normal/Fighting types: Hariyama, Linoone, Breloom, Aggron, Slaking.
HM07_Waterfall: Same as Surf carriers: Swampert, Walrein, Milotic, Sharpedo, Tentacruel.
HM08_Dive: Same as Surf carriers — most learn it. Dive's charge turn limits battle utility.
hm_compatibility.tip: The canonical 4-HM slave is Linoone (Cut / Surf / Strength / Rock Smash) with one battle slot for Return — covers 90% of overworld traversal.

battle_frontier.location: Artificial island reachable by ship from Lilycove (after Hall of Fame entry)
battle_frontier.currency: Battle Points (BP) earned from win streaks

facilities.name: Battle Tower
facilities.format: Choose 3 Pokemon, win 7 battles in a row, repeat
facilities.level: Lv 50 (with auto-level option) or Open Lv 100
facilities.tips: Use a balanced trio: physical wall, special wall, sweeper. Ban yourself from gimmicks.

facilities.name: Battle Dome
facilities.gimmick: You can scout enemy Pokemon's stats and moves before battle.
facilities.tips: Use Choice Band attackers - the format favors quick OHKOs.

facilities.name: Battle Palace
facilities.format: 3v3 single, but YOU don't pick moves - your Pokemon's Nature decides what they do each turn
facilities.gimmick: Aggressive natures attack, defensive ones heal/buff. RNG-heavy.
facilities.tips: Use Adamant/Modest natures with high BP single-purpose movesets.

facilities.name: Battle Arena
facilities.format: 1v1 with a 3-turn judging system - if both Pokemon are alive after 3 turns, judges decide winner by Mind/Skill/Body scores
facilities.gimmick: Switching is banned. Status moves are penalized.
facilities.tips: Use offensive natures and STAB moves only. Slaking shines here (Truant doesn't dock score).

facilities.name: Battle Factory
facilities.format: Rental Pokemon - you get a random pool, choose 3, then can swap with defeated opponents' Pokemon after each round
facilities.gimmick: You don't use your own Pokemon at all.
facilities.tips: Pick balanced typing on round 1. Always swap into a Pokemon that covers your previous loss.

facilities.name: Battle Pike
facilities.format: Choose a path through 14 rooms, each containing trainers, wild Pokemon, status traps, or NPCs
facilities.gimmick: Lottery-style; you pick from 3 doors blind. Avoid status floors with Lum Berry.
facilities.tips: Use a tank with Recover/Rest. Bring Lum Berries on every Pokemon.

facilities.name: Battle Pyramid
facilities.format: 7-floor dark maze with wild Pokemon, trainers, and items. Must find the boss on each floor with limited Flash range.
facilities.gimmick: Held items don't carry between floors - you find new ones in the maze.
facilities.tips: Use Pokemon with high HP and self-recovery (Leftovers found in maze). Map memorization helps.

post_game.no_sevii_islands: Note: Emerald does NOT have Sevii Islands - that's a FireRed/LeafGreen feature. Emerald's post-game equivalents are the Battle Frontier, Trainer Hill, and the legendary trio quest (Regis, roaming Latios/Latias, weather trio).
post_game.trainer_hill: Located near the Mauville-Verdanturf cable car route. 4-floor trainer rush - clear all rooms in the fastest time. The default has 3 fixed trainer themes (Variety, Unique, Strong). Records are saved as time stamps.

rematches.gym_leaders: Gym Leader rematches are NOT in vanilla Emerald (they're in HeartGold/SoulSilver). Hoenn rematches were added retroactively in Omega Ruby/Alpha Sapphire.
rematches.regular_trainers: Trainers across Hoenn give you their PokeNav numbers. Call them or wait for them to call you - they'll offer rematches with stronger teams. The strongest rematches are post-Hall of Fame and give massive EXP.

post_game_legendaries: Rayquaza (Sky Pillar - returns post-game if defeated)
Latios or Latias (roaming, the other via Eon Ticket)
post_game.steven_rematch: After becoming Champion, talk to Steven Stone in his Mossdeep house - he gives you a Beldum (eventual Metagross). He also has secret rematch dialogue in his house referencing Hoenn lore.
completion_goals: Catch all 386 Pokemon (requires trades with FireRed/LeafGreen and Ruby/Sapphire)
Earn all 14 Battle Frontier Symbols (Silver and Gold)
Beat Trainer Hill in record time

steven_rematch_full.trigger: Become Champion + complete the National Pokedex (Hoenn dex variant). Speak to Steven inside Meteor Falls (B1F deeper chamber, accessible via Surf + Waterfall after Sootopolis).
steven_rematch_full.actually_in_vanilla: VERIFY ON CART - vanilla Emerald has Steven appear at his Mossdeep house (gives Beldum). The Meteor Falls rematch is a DataMined unused event in some sources but is NOT a vanilla-cart confirmed encounter. ORAS adds a confirmed Meteor Falls Steven post-Delta-Episode.
steven_rematch_full.what_steven_actually_gives: In vanilla post-Hall-of-Fame Emerald: a Beldum (with Iron Defense + Take Down) at his Mossdeep house. That's the confirmed-on-cart interaction.

bp_shop.location: Battle Frontier mall - 3 vendors: held items, TMs, and stat-up items

stat_items.item: Protein / Iron / Carbos / Calcium / Zinc / HP Up
stat_items.note: Vitamins - cap at 100 EVs per stat. Cheaper than the Mart's $9,800.

move_tutor_bp_costs.location: Battle Frontier mall - move tutor NPC
move_tutor_bp_costs.note: Battle Frontier is the only place to legitimately learn most of these in Emerald. Defense Curl + Rollout combo, Substitute, and Vital Throw are the highest-value picks.

trainer_hill_full.structure: 4 floors x 4 trainers per floor = 16 trainers per run. Three switchable themes change which trainer roster you face.

themes.name: Variety
themes.description: Mixed types, default theme. Best for first clears and casual time trials.

themes.name: Unique
themes.description: Gimmick teams - mono-type trainers, status-heavy squads, double-battle pairs.

themes.name: Strong
themes.description: Highest-level competitive squads - typically Lv 50 with EV-trained-feel movesets. The serious time-trial theme.

themes.name: Expert
themes.description: Unlocks after a Strong run. Hardest tier; mirrors Frontier-tier movesets.

trainer_hill_full.rewards: No money or items from individual trainers - the prize is your time-stamp record. Saved per theme.
trainer_hill_full.speedrun_target: Sub-15-minute Variety is the casual benchmark; sub-10 on Strong is the speedrun target.

match_call_rematch_list.what: Most field trainers in Hoenn ask for your PokeNav number after the first fight. Post-Hall-of-Fame, they call you (or you call them) to schedule a rematch with a stronger team.

best_grind_targets.perks: Multiple Numel/Camerupt rematches; high cash payout.
Pays the most cash per rematch; only one Pokemon per fight.
Holds Nuggets that drop in rematches at low frequency.
Cash + EV chip from Skitty/Delcatty rematches.
5 trainers in a row near a Pokemon Center - most efficient grind in the cartridge. Repeatable indefinitely.
Strong team, decent payout, near Mauville for resupply.
Ghost rematches give Black Sludge and Spell Tag chances.

match_call_rematch_list.tracking: PokeNav Match Call screen shows green-dot icons next to trainers ready for rematches. Cycle by waiting in-game time or biking off-and-on the route.

roaming_repel_trick.what: Latios/Latias and (after Sootopolis) the wild Lv 40 Hoenn legendaries are roamers. They appear once per route then teleport.
roaming_repel_trick.trick: Equip a Lv 41+ Pokemon as your party lead and apply Max Repel. Repel blocks all wild encounters of LOWER level than your lead. The roamer is Lv 40, so it's the ONLY thing that can fire on the route.
roaming_repel_trick.tracking: Open the PokeNav -> Match Call -> roaming-marker map. The Lati's location is plotted as a flashing icon; Fly to a route adjacent to it, walk in, encounter, lock with Mean Look, attempt Master Ball / Net Ball.
roaming_repel_trick.rule: If the Lati flees during your encounter (turn-1 default), the marker moves to a new route. Save BEFORE entering the route so soft-reset puts the roamer back where you found it.

bikes.overview: Two bikes exist in Emerald: the Mach Bike (raw speed, slope climbing) and the Acro Bike (tricks, white rails, hopping tiles). You can only carry one at a time, but Rydel will swap them at no cost as often as you want.

acquisition.when: Free as soon as you reach Mauville (after the 2nd badge from Brawly). Pick either bike on first visit.
